front: What is the primary purpose of a Burndown Chart in Agile?
back: A Burndown Chart is a visual tool that tracks the amount of work remaining in a sprint or release against time. It helps the team monitor progress and predict if they will complete the work by the deadline.

front: Define a User Story in the context of Agile development.
back: A User Story is a simple, informal description of a software feature written from the perspective of the end-user. It follows the format: 'As a [type of user], I want [some goal] so that [some reason].' It is the primary unit of work in an Agile framework.

front: What is the role of a Product Backlog?
back: The Product Backlog is an ordered list of everything that is known to be needed in the product. It is the single source of requirements for any changes to be made to the product and is managed and prioritized by the Product Owner.

front: What is the difference between a Task Board and a Kanban Board?
back: A Task Board is a simple visual tool (often with columns like To Do, In Progress, Done) used primarily in Scrum to track sprint tasks. A Kanban Board is a more advanced tool used in Kanban to visualize workflow, limit work-in-progress (WIP), and manage flow; its columns represent specific stages of the process.

front: What is Velocity in Agile project management?
back: Velocity is a key metric that measures the average amount of work a team completes during a sprint, typically measured in story points or hours. It is used for forecasting and planning future sprints by providing an empirical basis for estimating how much work the team can likely handle.
